verbGlossary.COMPLEMENTS: The source {target} provides an alternative organizational view of the same construction scope as the target — one classifies by product/material, the other by function/system, and together they provide a complete picture
verbGlossary.CROSS-MAPS TO: Elements in {source} have defined crosswalk relationships to divisions in {target}, enabling translation between functional and product-based classification
verbGlossary.INCORPORATES: {source} includes {target} or facets, unifying it within a broader multi-table taxonomy
verbGlossary.ALIGNS WITH: The source {target} categories are designed to correspond with the target's hierarchy, enabling consistent cross-referencing between the two systems
verbGlossary.NUMBERED PER: {source} type uses {target} numbering scheme as its primary organizational identifier (e.g., specification sections numbered per MasterFormat)
verbGlossary.DERIVED FROM: {source} is created by selecting and adapting divisions and sections from {target} classification for company-specific use
verbGlossary.SEGMENTS: {source} already classified by {target} dimension (phase, location, category)
verbGlossary.ROLLS UP: {source} classified by {target} summary categories for executive reporting
verbGlossary.CLASSIFIES INCIDENTS FOR: {source} provides the cause-based {target} that feeds into the target's severity-based recordkeeping determination
verbGlossary.DETAILS INJURIES FOR: The source injury {target} provides granular nature/body-part/cause detail that enriches the target's severity-level recordkeeping with root-cause specificity
verbGlossary.REFINES: The source {target} adds finer-grained subcategories to the target's broader hazard types, enabling deeper pattern analysis within each hazard category
verbGlossary.REFERENCED BY: {source} is adopted by reference within {target} code, making compliance with the source a legal requirement when the target code is enforced
verbGlossary.DRIVES SCOPE IN: {target} code's occupancy and construction-type classifications determine which divisions and sections of {source} classification apply to a given project
verbGlossary.EXCEEDS: {source}requires performance levels that go beyond the minimum thresholds set by {target} standard, effectively using the code as a baseline
verbGlossary.AUGMENTS: {source}performance requirements (resilience, hazard resistance) not covered by {target}, supplementing code-minimum design with additional criteria
verbGlossary.EXTENDS: {source}of the target certification into additional performance dimensions (climate resilience, net-zero carbon) that the target does not fully address
verbGlossary.ADDS REQUIREMENTS TO: {source}ation requirements on top of what {target} already demands
verbGlossary.FORMALIZES: {source} document series provides the legal form and standard terms that give contractual effect to the target delivery method's risk and responsibility allocation
verbGlossary.PROVIDES METHODOLOGY FOR: {source} defines the calculation methods, classification rules, and recommended practices that inform how {target} structure is designed and applied
verbGlossary.GOVERNS WORKFLOW FOR: {source} documents define the submittal, review, approval, and rejection procedures that control how documents referencing {target} flow between parties
verbGlossary.DICTATES CONTENT OF: The source technical standard specifies the design criteria, installation methods, and testing procedures that must appear within the relevant target {target}
verbGlossary.MEASURES ADOPTION OF: {source} quantifies the percentage of projects or companies actively using {target} or classification, indicating data readiness and benchmarking viability
verbGlossary.BUILDS UPON: The source standard uses {target} baseline performance requirements as a foundation and adds occupant-health-focused criteria on top of them
verbGlossary.CODIFIES PROTECTIONS FOR: {source} that directly mitigate the hazard types classified by the target
verbGlossary.REFERENCES: {source} documents cite {target} system by name as the standard for organizing specifications, pay applications, and other contract administration forms
